# NotepadNext AppAI SPORE Package

This package is the portable seed form of the MantleOS NotepadNext assimilation.
It intentionally contains only the lifecycle SPORE, the lifecycle egg, and this
explanation.

## Files

- `notepadnext_appai_lifecycle_spore.png` - encrypted lifecycle SPORE PNG. The
  visible image is the public bootstrap; the embedded body payload is sealed.
- `notepadnext_appai_lifecycle_egg.json` - distilled NotepadNext AppAI body seed.
  It declares the resident identity, the C++/Qt/CMake host substrate, truths,
  commandments, app bands, mapped controls, AppAI terminal surface, VCW text
  commit policy, and primer/personality seed.
- `README.md` - this explanation inside the zip.

## How They Work Together

The egg is the readable body declaration. It says what the NotepadNext AppAI is,
which host it belongs to, which surfaces it may use, and which VCW bands it
expects. The SPORE is the lifecycle carrier. A SPORE-aware MantleOS runner reads
the bootstrap, derives the body key, opens the sealed payload, and uses the egg
plus payload data to recreate the assimilation workflow in a fresh work folder.

The package does not include the full working agent because that directory
contains cloned upstream source, runtime binaries, dependency trees, generated
reports, and lifecycle receipts. Those are reproducible outputs, not the compact
seed. Keeping the Git example to the seed package avoids storing a large binary
archive in the repository.

## Source Host Boundary

The upstream NotepadNext project remains read-only. MantleOS uses the SPORE/egg
to discover the host substrate, generate local evidence and adapters outside the
host repository, and only proposes insertion after explicit authorization.
